title: Chapter Forty-One: Counter Martial Arts
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The stream leaving the lakeside held the sun in its ripples, and the pleasure boat drifted on without stopping.

Only its owner had changed.

Yeon-shin sat cross-legged on the foredeck, listening to Master Won Jong speak. The old monk's voice was low, unhurried, carried on the early spring wind that moved through the trees lining the riverbank. Behind them the Deep Martial Alliance talents knelt in a row with their arms bound, silent as stones.

"I was about your age," Won Jong said. "Immature. A body like a piglet. Not like you, who already stands tall as a martial artist."

Yeon-shin said nothing. He watched the river.

"My family's prestige was high. My father served in the Forbidden City itself, until he was framed and exiled. Thanks to the emperor's mercy toward loyal subjects, we barely kept our lives." Won Jong paused. The wind stirred his grey robes. "Even that was a miracle."

"Then, at the place of exile?"

"The family became a martial house with broken ties. Suitable targets for the Blood Flame Sect coveting true energy." Won Jong's voice changed. Not louder. Harder. Like iron cooling in the forge. "When I returned from playing general with my friends, no one was alive. They said the Blood Flame Sect leader came personally. I wailed before my father's withered corpse."

Hatred. Unfaded by long years. Yeon-shin could feel it in the air between them, thick as incense smoke.

*The door rattled.*

He kept it shut.

"Fortunately my master caught my eye while I was begging for alms and I climbed Mount Song," Won Jong continued. "But even after becoming a monk, my afflictions didn't settle. I knew my own lack of talent. That's why I thought differently."

"Creating counter martial arts," Yeon-shin murmured.

Won Jong nodded.

"Let me restore martial arts imbued with the Buddha's dharma power. Martial arts solely for destroying the Blood Flame Sect. And have fellow disciples learn them. The talented Shaolin warrior monks would truly welcome it." The old monk's jaw tightened. "But there was affliction here too. Even if I created such arts, it was problematic. Wouldn't I be pushing my fellow disciples to their deaths? Against the Blood Flame Sect leader?"

Yeon-shin agreed. He had heard the Blood Flame Sect leader possessed supernatural powers. Though the Thirteen Heavens' leaders were said to rival those of the Nine Sects, in the vast world there were particularly powerful absolute beings -- the Blood Flame Sect leader, the Shaolin Temple abbot, and the Desolate Sect Divine Sword Squad Leader among them.

*Even making such martial arts would be problematic.*

The Shaolin abbot could not learn them personally and seek out the Blood Flame Sect leader. Such was the weight of one leading an orthodox sect at the martial world's peak. If defeated, irreversible ripples would spread.

"That's how life is," Yeon-shin said. "No matter how hard you run, the end is rarely within grasp. You can only climb step by step."

Sixteen years old, and speaking like an elder. Won Jong stared at him for a long moment, then turned his head back toward the river.

"Your enlightenment, having lived not even half of half my years, surpasses mine."

"My family too fell to the Blood Flame Sect. The Tyrant Sword Sect and its leader came together."

Silence. The river moved beneath them.

"You showed it," Won Jong said quietly. "The martial arts I was only grasping the beginnings of. I won't ask how. They say a martial artist's secrets aren't to be pried into. Now I'm curious about only one thing."

Yeon-shin turned his head to look at the old monk's profile. The nameless river flowing into the Yellow River had no visible end. Won Jong's figure gazing at the distant horizon seemed to speak of the life he had walked so far -- a monk and martial artist who had held onto just one affliction his entire life and could not let go.

"Will you tell me how I can help?" Won Jong said. "Just having martial arts that destroy evil and reveal righteousness emerge in the world would be enough. That achievement at that age. With your talent, you will surely reach the realm of supreme masters someday."

"Thank you for your words."

Yeon-shin brightened. He had been feeling the lack. With only Little Divine Monk Gak Jeong's briefly learned Buddhism, it was hard to refine the chants. Martial arts chants organized the nature of intent to be contained in true energy. Even the marketplace's Three Disasters Sword Technique manual contained phrases like "when cutting horizontally, think of cutting heaven and earth." That was what martial artists called enlightenment in internal force work -- finding more appropriate intent in operating accumulated power.

He could derive true energy operation methods from seeing most principles once. But the power would inevitably differ when intent ran deep.

Yeon-shin moved close to Won Jong.

"I believe you said it was the tenth chapter of the Diamond Sutra. Isn't there a passage in the Chapter on the Adornment of Pure Lands?"

"...?"

"There's a saying. 'One should develop a mind that dwells nowhere, yet brings forth that mind.'"

"What?"

"I'm requesting a dharma talk."

---

The pleasure boat flowed along the river, along time.

The Deep Martial Alliance talents did not row. Yeon-shin had gathered them to one side. He did not close off their dantian this time -- civilians were involved, and killing Sword Art Flower had been enough. The boatmen and women had already been put at risk.

*Cleanse the Six Senses purely. Make the mind dwell nowhere.*

He stood at the prow with his eyes closed and sank into meditation. Between sessions of Won Jong's dharma talk he was silent, and his silence instilled fear. He had taken the head of the locally famous Sword Art Flower in one strike. To the Deep Martial Alliance's young captives, it looked like the arrogance and confidence of one destined to lead the next generation of Desolate Sect.

He heard whispers. Talent mistakenly bestowed by heaven.

*Not wrong.*

He gave a bitter inward smile and let it pass.

"We'll get off here," he told the boatmen.

Won Jong had already disembarked. The Deep Martial Alliance talents lined up at the rail as if wanting to see them off, then hesitated. It was behaviour Yeon-shin had never seen -- crude, uncertain. Not rising talents at all.

*Not worthy of the title. The real ones are elsewhere.*

"What are you doing?" he said.

"Hm?"

"Trash wanting to stay with civilian people? Ridiculous. Get off."

Silence.

"We're going to the Righteous Heaven Sword Family together. I'll leave your punishment to Desolate Sect. You're lucky. You won't die right away."

He tied their arms behind them with arrest ropes, braiding thick straw gosak on the spot where his own ropes ran short. A technique learned as a Desolate Sect warrior. Won Jong watched with great satisfaction as Yeon-shin led the procession onto the main road.

Passersby tilted their heads as they passed. Some of the bound men hesitated, humiliation burning on their faces.

"Don't stop," Yeon-shin said. A sharp pulse of momentum -- the promise of swift death for any who ran. That was enough.

They had not gone far when a voice rang out.

"Aren't you the young masters of the Deep Martial Alliance!"

An escort bureau procession, coming up the road. The middle-aged man leading it approached with a slight smile that died the instant he saw the bound prisoners.

*The escort leader. Familiar with these men.*

"What -- what is this..."

"They committed crimes and were caught by Desolate Sect," Yeon-shin said, stepping forward. "These people are not free. Help is not needed. Please pass."

The man's face twisted into something strange. Yeon-shin took out his identity tablet -- the Desolate tablet, golden tree engraved, catching the sunlight.

He saw the calculation cross the man's features instantly. Free the Deep Martial Alliance young masters, curry favour. The nearby Deep Martial Alliance mattered more to a local escort than distant Desolate Sect in Hubei.

*Not everyone who associates with evil sects is like this. But this one is.*

The man's hand went to his sword. Yeon-shin's hand went to his.

"Escort Kim! What's happening?"

A young man emerged from a carriage in the middle of the stopped procession. The energy waves emanating from his body were clear and righteous -- unmistakable.

*The Righteous Heaven Sword Family's young master.*

"Heavens! I get to be the first in our family to meet Sir Lightning Flash!" The young man used lightness technique in his excitement, crossing the distance in a breath. "When I saw the dispatch about taking the Eight Fierce Rakshasa Sword's head, I knew! A person destined to become a dragon!"

"Are you the Righteous Heaven Sword Family's young master?" Yeon-shin asked, unmoved. "Are there more people from the main fortress at your house now?"

A hierarchy needed to be clear.

Different colours swirled in the young man's eyes, and his temperament instantly became calm.

"I am Hyeon Yu-ryang of the Righteous Heaven Sword Family." He clasped his fists and smiled. "Many are already waiting. They said with your arrival, all of Radiant Demon Wing who went out on missions would be gathered. A Shaolin monk is there too."

"Thank you for letting me know. Then if you'll excuse me."

Yeon-shin took out a flask from his bosom. He held it in his left hand and tilted it to pour water over his right.

The escort leader was still standing there. Still pretending.

"I still don't believe it," the man said. "Proving identity with just one tablet? Dwarf clan craftsmanship isn't exclusive to Desolate Sect!"

"The moment Young Master Hyeon stepped forward, energy waves were felt from your body. Very familiar." Yeon-shin's voice was flat. "You lot just can't hide it."

He operated the Fate Defying Scripture at full power. His form whirled around the man's flank in one stomping burst, the end of his words ringing out behind the target. His wet hand reached out and brushed through the man's hair.

Black water stained his palm. A bright red strand of hair was revealed.

Blood Flame Sect.

"This means a lot," Yeon-shin said calmly.

Chaos erupted among the escort bureau people. The Blood Flame Sect's doctrine that they could not hide their red hair was widely known -- and this man had found a way around it.

*If hiding hair is possible, they could send spies anywhere.*

Yeon-shin glanced at Won Jong.

"You said you thought about restoring Shaolin's Guanyin Blue Steel Hand. I want to include 'Blue Steel Hand' in the martial art's name. Would you permit it?"

"Amitabha." Won Jong raised one palm in banjang. "Rather, it would be a great honour."

"You worthless trash!" The revealed Blood Flame Sect member whirled around. His eyes met Yeon-shin's, and an ominous energy wave surged forth -- considerable, refined.

A Blood Swordmaster. Disguised. With dyed hair that had hidden him among ordinary men.

*Now it's meaningless how many there are.*

Yeon-shin slowly raised his hand. The energy flowing from his body was different from before -- an aspect like a Buddhist dharma king, calm and vast, flowing past the Blood Swordmaster's sinister momentum as it exploded outward.

"The trash is you," he said.

And moved his hand.

---
